articles.title: Dodgers Lead 2026 World Series Futures as Kalshi Data Highlights Top Contenders
articles.summary: The Dodgers sit atop 2026 World Series futures markets on Kalshi, giving early bettors a clear favorite nearly two years before the championship. This early line movement shows how prediction markets are expanding their time horizons, creating more opportunities for sharp money to find value before public sentiment catches up.

articles.title: Nike Quarterly Earnings Report: Polymarket Predictions
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are betting on Nike's upcoming quarterly earnings numbers, with prediction markets now covering individual company financial results. This marks another step toward prediction markets becoming the go-to venue for corporate earnings speculation, potentially offering better odds than traditional options trading for retail bettors.

articles.title: US Lawmakers Target Prediction Markets As Scrutiny Intensifies
articles.summary: US lawmakers are ramping up pressure on prediction markets with new regulatory threats and potential restrictions. The crackdown could force platforms to limit betting options or face operational hurdles that make markets less liquid and less profitable for traders.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Scaled to USD 21B in Monthly Volume in 2026 | TRM Blog
articles.summary: Prediction markets hit $21B in monthly volume during 2026, marking a massive scaling milestone for the industry. This volume surge signals prediction markets have moved from niche crypto curiosity to legitimate financial infrastructure that institutional players and serious money can't ignore anymore.

articles.title: Homophones, Plurals and Language ‘Rulescucks’ Are Roiling Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Grammar pedants are disrupting prediction markets by challenging bet resolutions based on technicalities like homophones, plural forms, and obscure linguistic rules. These "rulescucks" are creating resolution chaos that could undermine market credibility and scare away serious bettors who don't want their winnings held hostage by dictionary debates.

articles.title: Israeli Air Force major charged with using classified info to place bets on Polymarket
articles.summary: An Israeli Air Force major has been charged with leveraging classified military intelligence to place bets on Polymarket, turning state secrets into trading alpha. This marks the first known case of someone allegedly using insider government information to game prediction markets, which could trigger new regulations around who can bet on geopolitical events.

articles.title: A Valuation of Polymarket (POLY)
articles.summary: Someone's putting a price tag on Polymarket's native token POLY, attempting to figure out what the prediction platform is actually worth beyond its trading volume hype. The valuation matters because it signals whether prediction markets are building sustainable businesses or just riding the crypto gambling wave.

articles.title: Betting on Everything
articles.summary: The explosion of betting markets now covers everything from celebrity divorces to alien disclosure, turning formerly unbettable events into tradeable assets. This democratization of wagering creates new alpha opportunities for sharp bettors willing to research niche markets that bookmakers haven't properly priced yet.

articles.title: Prediction market bets on sports, election, war would be verboten under new legislation
articles.summary: New legislation would ban prediction market betting on sports, elections, and war-related events. The bill would gut the core categories that drive most prediction market volume and user engagement, potentially killing the industry's growth momentum.

articles.title: Moulton bans staff from using prediction markets
articles.summary: Rep. Seth Moulton banned his congressional staff from participating in prediction markets, likely responding to recent scrutiny over potential insider trading advantages that Capitol Hill employees might have when betting on political outcomes. The move signals growing concern among lawmakers about the ethics of political prediction markets, which could lead to broader regulatory crackdowns that would reshape the industry's access to Washington insiders.

articles.title: Opinion | Trump is right about polls — and the better bet
articles.summary: Trump's latest polling critique apparently contains some actual wisdom about survey methodology and accuracy. If he's genuinely onto something about polling flaws, that creates arbitrage opportunities where betting markets price in poll-driven narratives that don't reflect electoral reality.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Are Changing the Music Industry
articles.summary: Prediction markets are starting to let people bet on music outcomes, from Grammy winners to album sales figures to which artists will hit the charts. This creates new opportunities for entertainment bettors while giving record labels and artists real-time market intelligence on what the public actually expects from their releases.
